summary: Justin McCarthy's Feb 6 2026 narrative founding-document for StrongDM's Software Factory: charter rules, scenarios-as-holdout, satisfaction metric, Digital Twin Universe.
StrongDM's distilled doctrine: Seed -> Validation harness -> Feedback loop, with tokens as fuel; loop runs until holdout scenarios pass and stay passing.
Index of StrongDM's six recurring patterns (DTU, Gene Transfusion, Filesystem-as-memory, Shift Work, Semport, Pyramid Summaries) plus the code-as-opaque-artifact validation stance.
Documentation of Attractor as a graph-structured non-interactive coding agent (named nodes, LLM-evaluated natural-language edges, deterministic traversal) inside StrongDM's factory.
Deep-dive on StrongDM's Digital Twin Universe: behavioral clones of Okta/Jira/Slack/Google Docs/Drive/Sheets enabling thousands of scenarios per hour without rate limits or production risk.
StrongDM's official corporate blog announcement of the Software Factory pattern aimed at customers and executives, mirroring the technical site for a broader audience.
Pulumi vendor case adapting the StrongDM Dark Factory pattern to infrastructure-as-code: four-layer architecture, holdout BDD scenarios, Automation API/ESC/CrossGuard mapped onto Shapiro's levels.
HN comment subtree on StrongDM Attractor: bitwize calls it a 'horses to automobile' moment, lunar_mycroft and written-beyond push back on quality and economics.
HN comment subtree responding to StrongDM Attractor's spec-only release: practitioners try the spec on Claude/GPT, debate whether spec-rot is just code-rot relocated.
Show HN announcement and discussion of OctopusGarden, an open-source autonomous software factory implementing StrongDM-style holdout scenarios and probabilistic satisfaction.
OctopusGarden README: working open-source Dark Factory with Docker-isolated build, holdout scenarios, LLM-judge satisfaction scoring, and convergence loop.
Official strongdm/attractor GitHub repo: 6-7k lines of natural-language specifications and instructions to feed them to a coding agent of choice; no runtime code.
InfoQ reporting on Stripe's Minions: 1,300+ PRs/week, evolved from a fork of Block's Goose, blueprint-orchestrated, supporting $1T+ in annual payment volume; human-reviewed (Level 4).
Jamon Holmgren's first-person 'Night Shift' workflow: humans spec by day, agents code by night via AGENT_LOOP.md; claimed 5x speedup with better quality contingent on robust E2E tests.
Calvin French-Owen's Feb 17 2026 practitioner snapshot comparing Claude Code, Codex, Cursor: Opus for planning/orchestration, Codex for correctness; context discipline framed as the core skill.
